title: Biden-Harris Administration Announces Nearly $71 Million to Continue Strengthening America's Auto Communities, Expanding Battery Recycling, and Modernizing Manufacturing
content: As part of President Biden's Investing in America agenda, the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced $70.8 million to support partnerships with state and local governments, aimed at assisting small- and medium-sized manufacturers in sectors like automotive, battery recycling, and smart manufacturing. The funding will help upgrade manufacturing facilities, improve battery recycling, and modernize state-level manufacturing, with a focus on benefiting disadvantaged communities. This includes $50 million for retooling auto facilities in five states, $11.5 million to boost battery recycling efforts in seven states, and $9.3 million to accelerate smart manufacturing adoption across five states. The initiative supports the Biden-Harris Administrationâ€™s goals of strengthening domestic manufacturing and promoting clean energy.
author: Analysts Account
